title: Make It Yours: Create With AI

modules.title: The Blank Page Just Got a Partner
modules.sub: Discovering how AI tools fit into your creative process
modules.description: Learners explore a range of AI creative tools — for writing, art, and music — and experiment with them on a short creative challenge. They reflect on what changes when AI is in the room and start forming their own definition of what it means to be a creator.

modules.title: Who Owns What AI Makes?
modules.sub: Untangling copyright, credit, and creative ownership with AI
modules.description: Learners dig into real cases where ownership of AI-generated work has been disputed and examine the murky legal and ethical landscape around AI authorship. They make judgment calls about who deserves credit in different creative scenarios and begin building their own framework for giving credit fairly.

modules.title: Prompt Like a Pro Artist
modules.sub: Crafting prompts that shape AI output with intention and skill
modules.description: Learners practice writing and refining prompts across image, text, and audio tools to steer AI toward a specific creative vision. They compare outputs from weak versus strong prompts and discover how deliberate prompt choices are themselves a form of creative authorship.

modules.title: When AI Copies Without Knowing
modules.sub: Recognising how training data shapes — and sometimes steals — style
modules.description: Learners investigate how AI models are trained on existing human work and explore what happens when AI outputs closely echo a living artist's style or reproduce copyrighted material. They wrestle with the ethical question of whether unintentional copying is still a problem, and decide how that shapes their own use of these tools.

modules.title: Your Voice vs. AI's Voice
modules.sub: Keeping your creative identity when AI does some of the work
modules.description: Learners tackle a creative scenario where AI-generated content threatens to drown out their own perspective and style. They experiment with techniques for asserting their voice — editing, remixing, and constraining AI output — and draft an early version of their creator's statement explaining what makes their work distinctly theirs.

modules.title: Launch Your AI-Assisted Project
modules.sub: Finishing and reflecting on an original AI-assisted creative work
modules.description: Learners complete their AI-assisted creative project — a piece of writing, visual art, or music — and publish it alongside a polished creator's statement. The statement addresses their tool choices, authorship decisions, and the ethical trade-offs they navigated, demonstrating both creative skill and critical self-awareness.

synopsis: You're a creator with a deadline, a vision, and a powerful AI co-pilot â but every tool comes with a catch. This course puts you inside real creative dilemmas where you'll learn to use AI art, writing, and music tools effectively while grappling with the ownership, originality, and ethics they raise.
rationale: Teens are already using generative AI tools for creative work but lack frameworks for thinking about authorship, originality, and ethical use in their own creative practice.
learning_outcome: Students can produce an original AI-assisted creative project and write a reflective creator's statement that addresses authorship, tool choices, and the ethical decisions they made.
